texto: Agatha surveys the garden, its rows of crinkled spring cabbages and beanstalks entwining bowers of hawthorn and hazel. The rosemary is dotted with pale blue stars of blossom and chives nod heads of tousled purple. New sage leaves sprout silver green among the brittle, frost-browned remains of last year's growth. Lily of the valley, she thinks, that will be out in the cloister garden at Saint Justina's by now.
explicacao: Citação de Sarah Bower sobre esforço, persistência e resultados. “Agatha surveys the garden, its rows of crinkled spring cabbages and beanstalks entwining bowers o…” articula reflexao, flowers, spring em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como luto e spring.

frase_original: Agatha surveys the garden, its rows of crinkled spring cabbages and beanstalks entwining bowers of hawthorn and hazel. The rosemary is dotted with pale blue stars of blossom and chives nod heads of tousled purple. New sage leaves sprout silver green among the brittle, frost-browned remains of last year's growth. Lily of the valley, she thinks, that will be out in the cloister garden at Saint Justina's by now.

texto: One night when the moon was full, I explained to you about how the moon controls the tides, and you said I was like the moon and you were the sea, always following me about. And I said nothing, because I knew it was truly the other way around.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Sarah Bower: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “One night when the moon was full, I explained to you about how the moon controls the tides, and y…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que sucesso, historical-fiction, history pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de boa-noite e reflexao-noturna.
